question: In the NuScale design, is AC or DC power required for safety shutdown and cooling?

nuscale_ch01_0251.title: DSRS 8.3.1, Rev 0: AC Power Systems (Onsite)II.4 Compliance with GDC 17
nuscale_ch01_0251.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with various General Design Criteria (GDC) for onsite AC power systems. While the design conforms to some GDCs (such as GDC 2, 4, and 50), it departs from others (including GDC 17, 18, 33, and 34-44), for which NuScale supports exemptions or uses alternative principal design criteria. The document references specific sections for detailed explanations of these compliance positions.

nuscale_ch01_0252.title: DSRS 8.3.1, Rev 0: AC Power Systems (Onsite)II.5 Compliance with GDC 18
nuscale_ch01_0252.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with various General Design Criteria (GDC) for onsite AC power systems. While the design conforms to some GDCs (such as GDC 2, 4, and 50), it departs from others (including GDC 17, 18, 33, and 34-44), for which NuScale supports exemptions or uses alternative principal design criteria. The document references specific sections for detailed explanations of these compliance statements.

nuscale_ch01_0267.title: DSRS 8.3.2, Rev 0: DC Power Systems (Onsite)II.1 Conformance with RG 1.32
nuscale_ch01_0267.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for onsite DC power systems. It notes full compliance with some General Design Criteria (GDC), while for others (GDC 17, 18, 33, 34, 35, 38, 41, and 44), NuScale supports exemptions or alternative criteria. Specifically, conformance with Regulatory Guide (RG) 1.32 is only partial, applying to certain aspects of the Emergency DC Supply System (EDSS) design.

nuscale_ch01_0022.title: Engineered Safety Feature Materials
nuscale_ch01_0022.summary: The section describes how maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV) of the NuScale reactor provides multiple safety benefits, such as reducing corrosion, aiding leak detection, and minimizing combustible gases. The design relies on passive safety systems and a smaller fuel inventory, which together lower the risk of core damage and eliminate the need for external power during accidents, resulting in improved safety compared to traditional nuclear plants.

nuscale_ch01_0324.title: SRP 9.2.2, Rev 4: Reactor Auxiliary Cooling Water SystemII.4 Cooling Water System
nuscale_ch01_0324.summary: This section states that the NuScale Reactor Auxiliary Cooling Water System does not serve a safety-related cooling or accident mitigation function, so many regulatory criteria are not applicable. For applicable criteria, such as protection against natural phenomena and environmental/dynamic effects, the system partially conforms, with relevant design features and additional information referenced in other report sections.

nuscale_ch01_0269.title: DSRS 8.3.2, Rev 0: DC Power Systems (Onsite)II.3 Conformance with RG 1.81
nuscale_ch01_0269.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory criteria for onsite DC power systems. It details compliance or departures from General Design Criteria (GDC) and Regulatory Guides, noting that while NuScale generally conforms to several GDCs, it seeks exemptions from others (such as GDC 17, 18, and 33) and partially conforms to RG 1.32 for certain design aspects. The section also clarifies that some requirements are addressed through alternative principal design criteria.

nuscale_ch01_0079.title: Advanced and Evolutionary Light-Water Reactor Design Issues
nuscale_ch01_0079.summary: This section describes how the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report ensures compliance with regulatory criteria by incorporating relevant operational experience and NRC guidance, including reviews of NRC Bulletins, Generic Letters, and 10 CFR 21 notifications. It also addresses advanced and evolutionary light-water reactor design issues, referencing NRC policy and technical guidance to ensure all applicable licensing and safety requirements are met.

nuscale_ch01_0325.title: SRP 9.2.2, Rev 4: Reactor Auxiliary Cooling Water SystemII.5 Cooling Water System Inspection
nuscale_ch01_0325.summary: The section states that the NuScale site cooling water system does not serve a safety-related or accident mitigation function, so many regulatory criteria, including cooling water system inspection, are not applicable. For criteria that are partially applicable, such as protection against natural phenomena and environmental effects, the system conforms to requirements for non-safety-related systems and provides additional supporting information in referenced sections.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate the need for external power and large coolant loops, significantly lowering the risk of core damage and improving safety compared to traditional nuclear plants.

nuscale_ch01_0387.title: SRP 9.5.1.1, Rev 0: Fire Protection ProgramII.4 Fire Protection for Permanently Shutdown and Decommissioning Reactor Plants
nuscale_ch01_0387.summary: This section explains that the fire protection requirements for permanently shutdown and decommissioning reactor plants (SRP 9.5.1.1, II.4) are not applicable to the NuScale design, as these criteria only apply to reactor licensees who have submitted certifications for license termination under 10 CFR 50.82. The NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report clarifies that such requirements do not pertain to their design or current licensing stage.

nuscale_ch01_0278.title: SRP Appendix 8-A, Rev1: General Agenda, Station Site VisitsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0278.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to DC power systems and station blackout requirements. It notes that NuScale partially conforms to several regulatory guides, with some requirements not applicable due to the design’s passive safety features and the responsibilities assigned to the COL applicant. Additionally, SRP Appendix 8-A is identified as governing NRC staff site visits during licensing reviews, and is not directly applicable to the design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0275.title: DSRS 8.4, Rev 0: Station BlackoutII.1 Compliance with 10 CFR 50.63 and the guidelines of RG 1.155
nuscale_ch01_0275.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s compliance with regulatory requirements for station blackout (SBO) and related DC power systems. It notes that NuScale partially conforms to 10 CFR 50.63 and RG 1.155, as well as other related guidance, mainly due to the passive safety design that allows safety functions to be maintained without AC power for 72 hours after an SBO event. Some requirements, such as alternate AC power sources, are deemed not applicable to the NuScale design, and certain responsibilities are assigned to the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0129.title: 1.9.4 Operational Experience (Generic Communications)
nuscale_ch01_0129.summary: NuScale reviewed the Standard Review Plan (SRP) and related guidance to ensure their design meets NRC regulatory criteria, documenting any gaps and their resolutions. The company also assessed conformance with NRC requirements related to Three Mile Island and unresolved generic safety issues, incorporating relevant information and resolutions into their Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) as required by regulations.

nuscale_ch01_0412.title: SRP 9.5.6, Rev 3: Emergency Diesel Engine Starting SystemAll (II.1 thru II.4)Compliance with GDC 2, GDC 4, GDC 5, and GDC 17
nuscale_ch01_0412.summary: The NuScale plant design does not include or require safety-related emergency diesel generators or their associated systems, such as starting, fuel oil, cooling, lubrication, or air intake/exhaust systems. As a result, compliance with the referenced General Design Criteria (GDC) and Standard Review Plan (SRP) sections is not applicable, since no AC or DC power is relied upon for the performance of NuScale plant safety functions.

nuscale_ch01_0413.title: SRP 9.5.7, Rev 3: Emergency Diesel Engine Lubrication SystemAll (II.1 thru II.4) Compliance with GDC 2, GDC 4, GDC 5, and GDC 17
nuscale_ch01_0413.summary: The NuScale plant design does not require or include safety-related emergency diesel generators, and therefore the associated systems (fuel oil storage, cooling water, starting, lubrication, and air intake/exhaust) and their compliance with GDC 2, 4, 5, and 17 are not applicable. The plant does not rely on AC or DC power for the performance of its safety functions.
